content_of_post_to_comment:
If you read this to the end and give me your perspective, you would have helped someone greatly.

I'm 19, male, and this is basically what my whole life revolves around right now: confusion and an inability to make choices. I genuinely don't know what I want to do with my life.

I graduated high school at 16 and moved to Europe alone. It was a disaster. The years from 16 to 18 were rough. I don't fully know the damage I did to myself or how much potential and time I wasted, but the delusion that I "learned something" from it is basically the only thing keeping my ego intact.

I don't know what to do, and after highschool there was no clear thing I actually chose to do, it was always let me try this and see where it leads me after 6 months...I like everything ....psychology, reading people, math, science .. and I'm also a classically trained pianist. That's actually the problem.

I've been playing since I was 7. I was fairly talented and got a lot of praise for it, and I leaned on that praise to feel good about myself amid the bullying and other childhood-trauma stuff. Because of that, I've never been sure whether I actually love piano, or whether I just love the praise it got me.

Then I moved to Europe to study piano seriously, and it got worse and worse. I expect a lot from it, I've gotten worse with time instead of better, and I like it less the more I do it.

I did one year of math alongside piano, but it got too hard, so I dropped math completely to commit to piano as a career. But everytime with piano im like: If in 6 months i dont see myself gettign better, ill quit for good. Ive said that 6-7 times in 4 years. basically every 6 months

In hindsight, that decision looks cringe. Right now I'm genuinely repulsed by piano — if I had the choice, I wouldn't practice at all. My relationship with it is complicated to the point where I don't even know if I like it. And if I'm not sure I like something, doesn't that mean I don't? Except I don't fully trust that logic either — it sounds like one of those generic self-improvement one-liners that seeps in from mindless scrolling. (wtf does "if you're not sure you like it, you don't" even mean?)

On top of that, I have a long track record of telling myself "this time I'll actually stick to it" and then not doing it. AI projects, plans, whatever — I promise myself I'll follow through, and I don't. The number of times this has happened is insane, and it's exhausting. I like maths. I love it. Im not the best at it. But I love it. But when I imagine myself choosing it for good, then I get really scared and feel Im gonna regret it and also feel that initially I chose it for the wrong reasons and that maths isnt my thing, since really it was never my strong point. I dont know what my strong points were, I was bullied a lot and lived in constant fear. I wasnt able to shine purely. And im not victimizing here. (Probably am tho)

So now I'm stuck in a loop: if I quit piano for math, I'll probably regret it and feel suffocated by how rigid and square math is. If I keep going with piano, I feel unsafe, like I'm missing out on real knowledge, like I'm just entertainment for an audience of old people who might not even care. Then I second-guess that too, because classical music is obviously more than that, and it's probably not as useless as I'm making it sound in the moment.

I've basically lost any real reference point for how to make decisions. I litteraly just read biographies of business men and just start acting and behaving like Im gonna do and be like them, like i wanna be like this business man , because the aura of his biography just influenced me so bad....but in what world is my decision center so bad that it will get totally highjacked by a freaking boook??!?!?!? My dad isn't really available for this kind of thing, and my mom tries her best, but it's not really something she can solve for me either.

I'm sick of the delusion that I'm going to stick to something. There's a feeling in my chest that blocks me from deciding anything, and I don't know how to get rid of it. It feels like something completely absurd is dictating my life, keeping me stuck, wasting my time, and stopping me from actually living.

I don't know if I'm just cooked from bad habits, low dopamine, and constant distraction, or if there's something else going on. Any perspective helps. Is it just my low dopamine not letting me take a good decision? or the overflow of information making everything numb?

I took a 6 days no phone retreat in isolation, no stimuli for august. I dont know how this will help . maybe it will. Maybe ill come out of it with the delusion of having made a choice and then not stick to it 1 week after.
comment: The part about changing direction every 6 months really stood out to me. I wonder if you're trying to find a choice that removes uncertainty before committing, but every path has a period where it feels wrong. Also, piano being tied to childhood praise makes it hard to judge. Maybe don't ask "is this my calling?" but "can I choose this for the next year and learn from it?"
